title: Mildly Earthy Portuguese Cherry Pasta
description: This dish is a unique blend of earthy and crisp flavors, featuring cherries and a pasta water emulsion, making it a crowd-pleaser and ayurvedic sattvic friendly.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining the spaghetti.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the garlic and mushrooms until the mushrooms are tender.
Add the cherries to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice and cook for 1 minute.
Add the reserved pasta water to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked pappardelle pasta to the skillet, tossing to coat with the cherry and mushroom sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the pasta to a serving dish and top with grated parmesan cheese and chopped parsley.
